title: Classifying a quadrilateral
problem: A quadrilateral has two pairs of parallel sides, all four sides equal, and no right angles. Identify the quadrilateral.

steps.instruction: Check how many pairs of parallel sides there are.
steps.working: Two pairs of parallel sides means it belongs to the parallelogram family.

steps.instruction: Check whether all four sides are equal.
steps.working: All four sides are equal, so it is either a square or a rhombus.

steps.instruction: Check whether there are any right angles.
steps.working: There are no right angles, so it cannot be a square.
A quadrilateral with all sides equal, two pairs of parallel sides, and no right angles is a rhombus.

title: Finding a missing angle in a quadrilateral
problem: Three angles of a quadrilateral are $75°$, $110°$, and $95°$. Find the fourth angle.

steps.instruction: Recall the angle sum of a quadrilateral.
steps.working: The four angles of any quadrilateral add to $360°$.

title: Using algebra to find angles in a parallelogram
problem: A parallelogram has one angle of $(4x - 20)°$ and the opposite angle of $(2x + 40)°$. Find $x$ and the size of all four angles.

steps.instruction: Use the property that opposite angles in a parallelogram are equal.
Find the size of the angle given.
Find the adjacent angles using co-interior angles.
steps.working: Adjacent angles in a parallelogram are supplementary: $180° - 100° = 80°$
